questions.q: Narinder Singh Kapany is widely known as the 'Father of' which field?
questions.explanation: Kapany's pioneering work on transmitting light through glass fibres earned him the title 'Father of Fibre Optics'.

questions.q: In which country was Narinder Singh Kapany born?
questions.explanation: Kapany was born in India, in Moga in the Punjab region, in 1926.

questions.q: Kapany's work focused on transmitting what through thin glass fibres?
questions.explanation: His central achievement was demonstrating that light could be carried efficiently along bent glass fibres.

questions.q: The term 'fiber optics' is generally credited to which scientist?
questions.explanation: Kapany is credited with coining the term 'fiber optics' in an article he published in 1960.

questions.q: Fibre optic cables are most famous today for carrying which kind of signals over long distances?
questions.explanation: Optical fibres form the backbone of modern telecommunications, carrying internet, phone and TV data as pulses of light.

questions.q: In which broad branch of science did Narinder Singh Kapany primarily work?
questions.explanation: Kapany was a physicist who specialized in optics, the study of light and its behaviour.

questions.q: Besides being a scientist, Kapany was also notable as a:
questions.explanation: Kapany founded and led several technology companies, blending his scientific work with entrepreneurship.

questions.q: Optical fibres guide light by repeatedly bouncing it inside the fibre through a process called:
questions.explanation: Light stays trapped in a fibre because of total internal reflection at the boundary of the glass core.

questions.q: Kapany spent much of his professional career in which country, where he built his companies?
questions.explanation: Kapany emigrated to the United States, settling in California where he founded technology firms in Silicon Valley.

questions.q: The thin strands at the heart of fibre optics are made primarily of what material?
questions.explanation: Optical fibres are drawn from very pure glass (silica), with plastic fibres used in some applications.

questions.q: At which British institution did Kapany conduct his pioneering doctoral research on fibre optics?
questions.explanation: Kapany earned his PhD at Imperial College London, where he carried out his early fibre optics experiments.

questions.q: Kapany worked at Imperial College with which optics researcher on the landmark fibre bundle experiments?
questions.explanation: Kapany collaborated with Harold Hopkins on transmitting images through bundles of glass fibres.

questions.q: Kapany and Hopkins published a key 1954 paper in which journal demonstrating image transmission through fibre bundles?
questions.explanation: Their 1954 paper in Nature reported transmitting an image through an aligned bundle of glass fibres.

questions.q: Kapany's widely cited 1960 article that helped popularize the field appeared in which magazine?
questions.explanation: His 1960 Scientific American article on 'Fiber Optics' brought the term and the technology to a broad audience.

questions.q: In which decade did Kapany carry out the fibre optics work that established the field?
questions.explanation: Kapany's foundational experiments and the term 'fiber optics' date from the 1950s.

questions.q: One of the earliest major practical uses of fibre optic bundles that Kapany helped enable was in:
options: Medical endoscopes for viewing inside the body
questions.explanation: Flexible fibre bundles allowed doctors to see inside the body, leading to modern endoscopes.

questions.q: Kapany founded which pioneering fibre optics company in California in 1960?
questions.explanation: Kapany founded Optics Technology Inc. in 1960 to commercialize optics and fibre optics research.

questions.q: In approximately what year was Narinder Singh Kapany born?
questions.explanation: Kapany was born on 31 October 1926 in Moga, Punjab.

questions.q: Kapany was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours, in what year?
questions.explanation: Kapany was honoured with the Padma Vibhushan in 2021, awarded posthumously, recognizing his contributions.

questions.q: A 1999 Fortune magazine feature on 'unsung heroes' of the 20th century highlighted Kapany for his role in:
questions.explanation: Fortune named Kapany among the unsung heroes of the century for his foundational work in fibre optics.

questions.q: Kapany was a noted collector and patron of art from which community, to which he belonged?
questions.explanation: Kapany, a Sikh, was a major collector and promoter of Sikh art and helped establish Sikh art collections in museums.

questions.q: What did Kapany's teacher reportedly tell him in school that he set out to disprove, inspiring his career?
options: That light only travels in straight lines
That sound cannot travel in water
questions.explanation: A schoolteacher's claim that light travels only in straight lines spurred Kapany to show light could be guided along curved fibres.

questions.q: In an optical fibre, the central light-carrying region is surrounded by a layer with a lower refractive index called the:
questions.explanation: The cladding has a lower refractive index than the core, enabling total internal reflection that keeps light confined.

questions.q: The 2009 Nobel Prize in Physics for fibre optic communication was awarded to which scientist, sometimes contrasted with Kapany's earlier work?
questions.explanation: Charles K. Kao won the 2009 Nobel Prize for low-loss optical fibres; Kapany's earlier foundational role was widely noted but unawarded.

questions.q: Kapany endowed a chair in optoelectronics and supported Sikh studies at which University of California campus?
questions.explanation: Kapany endowed positions at UC Santa Cruz, including support for optoelectronics and Sikh and Punjab studies.

questions.q: Kapany authored an influential 1967 textbook with which title that helped define the field?
questions.explanation: His 1967 book 'Fiber Optics: Principles and Applications' became a foundational text in the discipline.

questions.q: Kapany later founded which company, reflecting his ongoing entrepreneurship in optical technologies?
questions.explanation: Kapany founded Kaptron Inc., one of several companies he started to develop fibre optic components.

questions.q: In which city in Punjab was Narinder Singh Kapany born?
questions.explanation: Kapany was born in Moga, in the Punjab region of India.

questions.q: A practical challenge that limited early long-distance fibre optics, later overcome with ultra-pure glass, was high:
questions.explanation: Early fibres lost too much light over distance; reducing attenuation with purer glass made long-haul optical communication possible.

questions.q: In which year did Narinder Singh Kapany pass away?
questions.explanation: Kapany died in December 2020 at the age of 94 in California.
